---
title: "Information Systems: Components & Architecture"
subtitle: "From **users** and **apps** to **servers**, **algorithms**, and **data**"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.07
    center: false
    code-overflow: wrap
    controls: true
    progress: true
    html-math-method: katex
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.5.2/css/all.min.css">
    <style>
      :root {
        --ink: #172033;
        --muted: #59657a;
        --blue: #2563eb;
        --cyan: #0891b2;
        --green: #059669;
        --amber: #d97706;
        --rose: #e11d48;
        --violet: #7c3aed;
        --bg: #fbfcff;
        --soft: #eef5ff;
        --line: #d7e2f2;
      }
      .reveal { font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; color: var(--ink); }
      .reveal h1, .reveal h2, .reveal h3 { letter-spacing: -0.035em; color: var(--ink); }
      .reveal h1 { font-size: 2.35em; }
      .reveal h2 { font-size: 1.65em; margin-bottom: 0.55em; }
      .reveal h3 { font-size: 1.05em; }
      .reveal strong { color: var(--blue); font-weight: 800; }
      .reveal p, .reveal li { font-size: 0.72em; line-height: 1.32; }
      .reveal ul { margin-top: 0.15em; }
      .reveal li { margin: 0.22em 0; }
      .subtitle { color: var(--muted); }
      .muted { color: var(--muted); }
      .small { font-size: 0.64em !important; }
      .tiny { font-size: 0.52em !important; }
      .accent { color: var(--blue); }
      .big-idea {
        margin-top: 0.9em;
        padding: 0.8em 1em;
        border-left: 8px solid var(--blue);
        background: linear-gradient(90deg, #eef5ff 0%, #ffffff 80%);
        border-radius: 14px;
      }
      .icon-row, .icon-grid {
        display: grid;
        gap: 0.72rem;
        margin-top: 0.6rem;
      }
      .icon-row { grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); }
      .icon-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); }
      .mini-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 0.75rem; margin-top: 0.6rem; }
      .tile {
        border: 1px solid var(--line);
        border-radius: 18px;
        padding: 0.72rem 0.82rem;
        background: rgba(255,255,255,0.92);
        box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(23, 32, 51, 0.06);
      }
      .tile i { font-size: 1.18rem; color: var(--blue); margin-right: 0.42rem; }
      .tile .label { font-size: 0.74em; font-weight: 800; color: var(--ink); }
      .tile .desc { font-size: 0.50em; color: var(--muted); margin-top: 0.25rem; line-height: 1.2; }
      .callout {
        border-radius: 18px;
        background: #f8fbff;
        border: 1px solid var(--line);
        padding: 0.8rem 1rem;
      }
      .term-strip {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.45rem;
        margin-top: 0.55rem;
      }
      .term-strip span {
        border: 1px solid var(--line);
        border-radius: 999px;
        padding: 0.25rem 0.55rem;
        background: #ffffff;
        font-size: 0.50em;
        font-weight: 750;
      }
      .two-col { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 0.95fr 1.05fr; gap: 1.1rem; align-items: start; }
      .three-col { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 0.8rem; }
      .layer-title { font-size: 0.82em; font-weight: 850; margin-bottom: 0.25rem; }
      .layer-note { font-size: 0.52em; color: var(--muted); line-height: 1.2; }
      .tag-blue { color: var(--blue); }
      .tag-cyan { color: var(--cyan); }
      .tag-green { color: var(--green); }
      .tag-amber { color: var(--amber); }
      .tag-violet { color: var(--violet); }
      .footnote { position: absolute; bottom: 20px; left: 65px; right: 65px; font-size: 0.45em; color: var(--muted); }
      .mermaid { font-size: 0.86em; }
      .reveal pre code { max-height: 520px; }
      section.slide.level2 { background: var(--bg); }
    </style>
---

## Learning goal

<div class="big-idea">
By the end, you should be able to explain how **people**, **apps**, **networks**, **servers**, **programs**, **algorithms**, and **data stores** work together inside an **information system**.
</div>

<div class="term-strip">
<span><i class="fa-solid fa-user"></i> <strong>people</strong></span>
<span><i class="fa-solid fa-display"></i> <strong>clients</strong></span>
<span><i class="fa-solid fa-globe"></i> <strong>HTTP/TCP</strong></span>
<span><i class="fa-solid fa-server"></i> <strong>servers</strong></span>
<span><i class="fa-solid fa-code"></i> <strong>programming</strong></span>
<span><i class="fa-solid fa-diagram-project"></i> <strong>algorithms</strong></span>
<span><i class="fa-solid fa-database"></i> <strong>data</strong></span>
</div>

::: {.notes}
Use this opening to frame the session as one connected picture, not a list of disconnected terms.
:::

## The large picture

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"primaryColor": "#EEF5FF", "primaryTextColor": "#172033", "primaryBorderColor": "#9BB8E8", "lineColor": "#6B7C93", "secondaryColor": "#ECFEFF", "tertiaryColor": "#F0FDF4"}, "flowchart": {"htmlLabels": true, "curve": "basis"}} }%%
flowchart LR
  U["👥<br/><b>People / Users</b>"]
  C["🖥️📱<br/><b>Client / Frontend</b><br/>UI · CLI · Browser · Apps"]
  N["🌐<br/><b>Network</b><br/>HTTP/HTTPS over TCP/IP"]
  S["🧠🖧<br/><b>Server / Backend</b><br/>Programs · Algorithms · Rules"]
  D["🗄️<br/><b>Data Layer</b><br/>Databases · Files · Cloud"]
  O["📊<br/><b>Outputs</b><br/>Reports · Decisions · Actions"]

  U --> C
  C -->|request| N
  N --> S
  S -->|read / write| D
  D --> S
  S -->|response| N
  N --> C
  C --> O

  classDef user fill:#fff7ed,stroke:#fdba74,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef client fill:#eff6ff,stroke:#93c5fd,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef net fill:#ecfeff,stroke:#67e8f9,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef server fill:#f5f3ff,stroke:#c4b5fd,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef data fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#86efac,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef output fill:#fff1f2,stroke:#fda4af,stroke-width:2px;
  class U user;
  class C client;
  class N net;
  class S server;
  class D data;
  class O output;
```

## Major components

<div class="icon-grid">
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-microchip"></i><strong>Hardware</strong></span><div class="desc">Devices: laptops, phones, servers, routers, storage.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-window-restore"></i><strong>Software</strong></span><div class="desc">Operating systems, applications, services, tools.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-database"></i><strong>Data</strong></span><div class="desc">Facts stored as records, files, events, logs.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-users"></i><strong>People</strong></span><div class="desc">Users, managers, developers, admins, support.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-route"></i><strong>Processes</strong></span><div class="desc">Workflows, policies, procedures, approvals.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-network-wired"></i><strong>Networks</strong></span><div class="desc">Internet, LAN, Wi‑Fi, APIs, secure channels.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-shield-halved"></i><strong>Security</strong></span><div class="desc">Access control, encryption, backups, audit logs.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-code"></i><strong>Programming</strong></span><div class="desc">Code that defines system behavior and automation.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-diagram-project"></i><strong>Algorithms</strong></span><div class="desc">Step-by-step logic for solving business problems.</div></div>
</div>

## Core information flow

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"primaryColor": "#EEF5FF", "primaryTextColor": "#172033", "primaryBorderColor": "#93C5FD", "lineColor": "#64748B"}, "flowchart": {"htmlLabels": true, "curve": "linear"}} }%%
flowchart LR
  A["⌨️<br/><b>Input</b><br/>forms · commands · events"] -->
  B["⚙️<br/><b>Processing</b><br/>rules · programs · algorithms"] -->
  C["🗄️<br/><b>Storage</b><br/>database · files · logs"] -->
  D["📤<br/><b>Output</b><br/>reports · dashboards · alerts"] -->
  E["🔁<br/><b>Feedback</b><br/>corrections · decisions · actions"]
  E -. improves .-> A

  classDef input fill:#eff6ff,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef process fill:#f5f3ff,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef store fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#4ade80,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef output fill:#fff1f2,stroke:#fb7185,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef feedback fill:#fffbeb,stroke:#f59e0b,stroke-width:2px;
  class A input;
  class B process;
  class C store;
  class D output;
  class E feedback;
```

<div class="callout small">
An **information system** turns **input** into useful **output** through **processing**, **storage**, and **feedback**.
</div>

## Client side: where interaction begins

<div class="two-col">
<div>

- **Client** = user-facing device or program.
- **Frontend** = visible interaction layer.
- **UI** = visual controls: buttons, forms, menus.
- **CLI** = text commands in a terminal.
- **Browser** = web client for websites and web apps.
- **Apps** = mobile, desktop, or web software.

</div>
<div class="mini-grid">
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-window-maximize"></i><strong>Browser</strong></span><div class="desc">Loads pages, runs frontend code, sends HTTP requests.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-mobile-screen"></i><strong>Mobile app</strong></span><div class="desc">Native client with touch UI and device features.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-terminal"></i><strong>CLI</strong></span><div class="desc">Fast, scriptable text-based interaction.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-desktop"></i><strong>Desktop app</strong></span><div class="desc">Local interface that may also call backend services.</div></div>
</div>
</div>

## Client–server pattern

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"actorBorder": "#93c5fd", "actorBkg": "#eff6ff", "activationBorderColor": "#7c3aed", "activationBkgColor": "#f5f3ff", "sequenceNumberColor": "#172033", "signalColor": "#334155", "signalTextColor": "#172033"}} }%%
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant User as 👤 User
  participant Client as 🖥️ Client / Frontend
  participant Network as 🌐 HTTP(S) over TCP/IP
  participant Server as 🖧 Server / Backend
  participant Data as 🗄️ Database

  User->>Client: Clicks, types, or runs command
  Client->>Network: HTTP request
  Network->>Server: Delivers request packets
  Server->>Server: Run program + algorithm
  Server->>Data: Read / write data
  Data-->>Server: Return records
  Server-->>Network: HTTP response
  Network-->>Client: Deliver response packets
  Client-->>User: Show result
```

<div class="callout small">
The **client** asks; the **server** processes; the **network** carries messages; the **database** remembers.
</div>

## Network layer: HTTP and TCP/IP

<div class="two-col">
<div>

- **TCP/IP** moves data across networks in packets.
- **HTTP** defines web-style requests and responses.
- **HTTPS** = **HTTP** plus encryption.
- **APIs** expose backend capabilities to clients.
- **Internet**, **LAN**, and **Wi‑Fi** provide paths.

</div>
<div>

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"primaryColor": "#EEF5FF", "primaryTextColor": "#172033", "primaryBorderColor": "#93C5FD", "lineColor": "#64748B"}, "flowchart": {"htmlLabels": true, "curve": "linear"}} }%%
flowchart TB
  A["🌍<br/><b>Application Protocols</b><br/>HTTP · HTTPS · APIs"] -->
  B["📦<br/><b>Transport</b><br/>TCP: reliable delivery"] -->
  C["🧭<br/><b>Internet Layer</b><br/>IP: addressing + routing"] -->
  D["📡<br/><b>Network Access</b><br/>Wi‑Fi · Ethernet · Cellular"]

  classDef app fill:#eff6ff,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef tcp fill:#ecfeff,stroke:#22d3ee,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef ip fill:#f5f3ff,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef access fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#4ade80,stroke-width:2px;
  class A app;
  class B tcp;
  class C ip;
  class D access;
```

</div>
</div>

## Server side: where rules live

<div class="two-col">
<div>

- **Server** = machine or service that handles requests.
- **Backend** = hidden application logic.
- **Programming** creates backend behavior.
- **Algorithms** make decisions step by step.
- **Business logic** enforces organizational rules.
- **Authentication** verifies identity.
- **Validation** checks input before storage.

</div>
<div>

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"primaryColor": "#F5F3FF", "primaryTextColor": "#172033", "primaryBorderColor": "#A78BFA", "lineColor": "#64748B"}, "flowchart": {"htmlLabels": true, "curve": "basis"}} }%%
flowchart TB
  R["📥<br/><b>Request</b>"] --> A["🔐<br/><b>Authenticate</b>"]
  A --> V["✅<br/><b>Validate</b>"]
  V --> L["🧠<br/><b>Business Logic</b>"]
  L --> G["🔢<br/><b>Algorithm</b>"]
  G --> T["🧾<br/><b>Transaction</b>"]
  T --> X["📤<br/><b>Response</b>"]

  classDef node fill:#f5f3ff,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:2px;
  class R,A,V,L,G,T,X node;
```

</div>
</div>

## Data layer: persistent memory

<div class="three-col">
<div class="tile">
  <div class="layer-title"><i class="fa-solid fa-database tag-green"></i> <strong>Database</strong></div>
  <div class="layer-note">Structured records: customers, orders, products, accounts.</div>
</div>
<div class="tile">
  <div class="layer-title"><i class="fa-solid fa-file-lines tag-blue"></i> <strong>Files</strong></div>
  <div class="layer-note">Documents, images, exports, configuration files.</div>
</div>
<div class="tile">
  <div class="layer-title"><i class="fa-solid fa-cloud tag-cyan"></i> <strong>Cloud storage</strong></div>
  <div class="layer-note">Scalable storage managed over network services.</div>
</div>
<div class="tile">
  <div class="layer-title"><i class="fa-solid fa-warehouse tag-amber"></i> <strong>Data warehouse</strong></div>
  <div class="layer-note">Historical data organized for analytics and reporting.</div>
</div>
<div class="tile">
  <div class="layer-title"><i class="fa-solid fa-list-check tag-violet"></i> <strong>Logs</strong></div>
  <div class="layer-note">Events used for debugging, monitoring, and auditing.</div>
</div>
<div class="tile">
  <div class="layer-title"><i class="fa-solid fa-shield-halved tag-green"></i> <strong>Controls</strong></div>
  <div class="layer-note">Permissions, encryption, backups, retention, recovery.</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="callout small">
The **data layer** protects system memory: what happened, what exists now, and what can be analyzed later.
</div>

## Common architecture: three tiers

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"primaryColor": "#EEF5FF", "primaryTextColor": "#172033", "primaryBorderColor": "#93C5FD", "lineColor": "#64748B"}, "flowchart": {"htmlLabels": true, "curve": "basis"}} }%%
flowchart TB
  subgraph P["<b>Presentation Tier</b>"]
    UI["🎨 <b>UI</b>"]
    CLI["⌨️ <b>CLI</b>"]
    Browser["🌐 <b>Browser</b>"]
    App["📱 <b>Apps</b>"]
  end

  subgraph A["<b>Application Tier</b>"]
    API["🔌 <b>API</b>"]
    Logic["🧠 <b>Business Logic</b>"]
    Algo["🔢 <b>Algorithms</b>"]
    Auth["🔐 <b>Security</b>"]
  end

  subgraph D["<b>Data Tier</b>"]
    DB["🗄️ <b>Database</b>"]
    Files["📄 <b>Files</b>"]
    WH["📊 <b>Warehouse</b>"]
  end

  UI --> API
  CLI --> API
  Browser --> API
  App --> API
  API --> Logic
  Logic --> Algo
  Logic --> Auth
  Logic --> DB
  Logic --> Files
  Logic --> WH

  classDef presentation fill:#eff6ff,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef appTier fill:#f5f3ff,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef dataTier fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#4ade80,stroke-width:2px;
  class UI,CLI,Browser,App presentation;
  class API,Logic,Algo,Auth appTier;
  class DB,Files,WH dataTier;
```

<div class="term-strip">
<span><strong>Frontend</strong> ≈ presentation tier</span>
<span><strong>Backend</strong> ≈ application tier</span>
<span><strong>Database</strong> ≈ data tier</span>
</div>

## Frontend vs backend

<div class="two-col">
<div class="callout">

### <i class="fa-solid fa-palette"></i> **Frontend**

- **Visible** to the user.
- Handles **layout**, **forms**, **navigation**, and **feedback**.
- Runs in a **browser**, **mobile app**, **desktop app**, or **CLI**.
- Sends **requests** to the backend.

</div>
<div class="callout">

### <i class="fa-solid fa-gears"></i> **Backend**

- Usually **hidden** from the user.
- Handles **rules**, **security**, **processing**, and **data access**.
- Runs on **servers** or **cloud services**.
- Sends **responses** back to clients.

</div>
</div>

<div class="big-idea small">
**Frontend** asks: “What should the user see or do?”  
**Backend** asks: “What should the system calculate, enforce, store, or return?”
</div>

## Programming and algorithms

<div class="two-col">
<div>

- **Programming** turns requirements into executable **software**.
- **Programs** connect **UI**, **network**, **backend**, and **data**.
- **Algorithms** are precise steps for solving a task.
- Good systems separate **presentation**, **logic**, and **storage**.

</div>
<div>

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"primaryColor": "#FFF7ED", "primaryTextColor": "#172033", "primaryBorderColor": "#FDBA74", "lineColor": "#64748B"}, "flowchart": {"htmlLabels": true, "curve": "linear"}} }%%
flowchart TB
  Problem["❓<br/><b>Problem</b><br/>Approve a login"] --> Steps["🧩<br/><b>Algorithm</b><br/>Check user → compare password → allow/deny"]
  Steps --> Code["💻<br/><b>Program</b><br/>Implemented in code"]
  Code --> System["🏗️<br/><b>Information System</b><br/>Runs inside frontend/backend/data architecture"]

  classDef node fill:#fff7ed,stroke:#fdba74,stroke-width:2px;
  class Problem,Steps,Code,System node;
```

</div>
</div>

## Example: online shopping transaction

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"actorBorder": "#93c5fd", "actorBkg": "#eff6ff", "activationBorderColor": "#059669", "activationBkgColor": "#f0fdf4", "signalColor": "#334155", "signalTextColor": "#172033"}} }%%
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant Customer as 🧑 Customer
  participant App as 🛒 Browser / App
  participant API as 🔌 Backend API
  participant Logic as 🧠 Order Logic
  participant DB as 🗄️ Database
  participant Pay as 💳 Payment Service

  Customer->>App: Clicks “Buy Now”
  App->>API: HTTPS request
  API->>Logic: Start order workflow
  Logic->>DB: Check inventory + customer data
  DB-->>Logic: Return records
  Logic->>Pay: Authorize payment
  Pay-->>Logic: Payment result
  Logic->>DB: Save order + update stock
  Logic-->>API: Confirmation data
  API-->>App: HTTPS response
  App-->>Customer: Receipt + order status
```

## Relationship map

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"theme": "base", "themeVariables": {"primaryColor": "#EEF5FF", "primaryTextColor": "#172033", "primaryBorderColor": "#93C5FD", "lineColor": "#64748B"}, "flowchart": {"htmlLabels": true, "curve": "basis"}} }%%
flowchart LR
  IS["🏗️<br/><b>Information System</b>"]

  IS --> People["👥 <b>People</b>"]
  IS --> Tech["🧰 <b>Technology</b>"]
  IS --> Data["🗄️ <b>Data</b>"]
  IS --> Process["🔁 <b>Processes</b>"]
  IS --> Security["🛡️ <b>Security</b>"]

  Tech --> Hardware["🔌 <b>Hardware</b>"]
  Tech --> Software["💻 <b>Software</b>"]
  Tech --> Network["🌐 <b>Networks</b>"]

  Software --> Frontend["🎨 <b>Frontend</b>"]
  Software --> Backend["⚙️ <b>Backend</b>"]
  Software --> Apps["📱 <b>Apps</b>"]

  Frontend --> UI["🖱️ <b>UI</b>"]
  Frontend --> CLI["⌨️ <b>CLI</b>"]
  Frontend --> Browser["🌍 <b>Browser</b>"]

  Network --> HTTP["📨 <b>HTTP/HTTPS</b>"]
  Network --> TCP["📦 <b>TCP/IP</b>"]

  Backend --> Server["🖧 <b>Server</b>"]
  Backend --> Program["💻 <b>Programming</b>"]
  Backend --> Algorithm["🔢 <b>Algorithms</b>"]
  Backend --> Database["🗄️ <b>Database Access</b>"]

  classDef core fill:#fff7ed,stroke:#fdba74,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef branch fill:#eff6ff,stroke:#93c5fd,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef sub fill:#f8fafc,stroke:#cbd5e1,stroke-width:1.5px;
  class IS core;
  class People,Tech,Data,Process,Security branch;
  class Hardware,Software,Network,Frontend,Backend,Apps,UI,CLI,Browser,HTTP,TCP,Server,Program,Algorithm,Database sub;
```

## Mini-check: trace a login

<div class="two-col">
<div>

When a user logs in, identify each part:

1. User types credentials into the **UI**.
2. The **client** sends an **HTTPS** request.
3. **TCP/IP** carries the packets.
4. The **server/backend** receives the request.
5. A **program** runs a login **algorithm**.
6. The **database** checks the account record.
7. The **client** shows success or failure.

</div>
<div class="tile">
  <div class="layer-title"><i class="fa-solid fa-key tag-amber"></i> <strong>Login algorithm</strong></div>
  <div class="layer-note">
  Receive input → validate format → find account → compare password securely → create session → return result.
  </div>
</div>
</div>

## Key takeaways

<div class="icon-row">
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-layer-group"></i><strong>Architecture</strong></span><div class="desc">Most systems separate presentation, application logic, and data.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-handshake-angle"></i><strong>Client–server</strong></span><div class="desc">Clients request; servers process; responses return through the network.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-code"></i><strong>Programming</strong></span><div class="desc">Code implements workflows, rules, security, and algorithms.</div></div>
  <div class="tile"><span class="label"><i class="fa-solid fa-database"></i><strong>Data</strong></span><div class="desc">Databases, files, logs, and warehouses preserve system memory.</div></div>
</div>

<div class="big-idea small">
A modern **information system** is a connected environment: **people** use **clients** over **networks** to reach **servers** running **programs** and **algorithms** that manage **data** and produce useful **outputs**.
</div>
